title: Consultation on the proposed phased implementation of the incorporated by reference list for the Ministerial Reliance Order - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation on the proposed phased implementation of the incorporated by reference list for the Ministerial Reliance Order

## Current status: Open
Opened on May 5, 2026 and will close to  new input on June 4, 2026
Health  Canada is seeking feedback on the proposed phased implementation of the incorporated  by reference (IbR) list under the proposed Ministerial Reliance Order (Order).  This includes feedback on the development and updating of the IbR list, as well  as the high-level categories proposed for Phase 1.
We  welcome input until June 4, 2026.
Read  the proposed  phased implementation of the incorporated by reference list for the Ministerial  Reliance Order notice to learn more about  the proposed Order and how the IbR list would be developed and updated.

## Join in: How to participate
You can provide your feedback in  the following ways:
Send an email to lrm.consultations-mlr@hc-sc.gc.ca with  your ideas or comments to make yourself heard.
Send a letter with your ideas  and input to the address in contact us below.

## Who is the focus of this consultation
We will engage with:
- health system partners
- health care professionals
- interested members of the public
- patient involvement organizations
- livestock producers and associations
- industry and industry associations (human and  veterinary)

## Key questions for discussion
Through this consultation, Health Canada is seeking feedback  on:
- the       proposed approach to developing and updating the IbR list
- the       proposed categories for inclusion in Phase 1 and
- considerations       that should inform future phases of the IbR list
Input received will be used  to inform next steps related to the management of the list, including the  approach to consultation for Phase 2 of the IbR list. This will help ensure  that the policy for populating the IbR list is informed by and responsive to  industry, downstream partners such as provinces and territories, other  interested stakeholders and the public.
Health Canada 150 Tunney's Pasture Driveway Ottawa, Ontario K1A 0K9 Address locator: 302B
Email: lrm.consultations-mlr@hc-sc.gc.ca
